rulings.Case Title: JEREMIAH CASEY VS. 1875 MISSION STREET PARTNERS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 18, 2017 line 1. DEFENDANT CORVORN, LLC, 200 DOLORES STREET, LLC, 1801 MISSION STREET, LLC, 1863 MISSION STREET, LLC, MICHAEL MAMONE, DOLORES & 15TH STREET PARTNERS, LLC, JOSEPH HEGE, 251 ARKANSAS, LLC, 806-808 DOLORES, LLC, 1875 MISSION STREET, LLC, JEREMIAH CULLINANE, AMELIA RITZ, LLC MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH Amended COMPLAINT GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. As to reformation of the contract, the motion is GRANTED. Plaintiff has agreed to strike the words "so that Plaintiff's share of any distributions from the projects located at 1875 Mission Street and 200 Dolores Street is based on his share of contributions to that project as a percentage of the combined contributions to that project made by him, Subrick and McCormick." As to attorney's fees, the motion is DENIED. Plaintiff need not allege the attorney fee provision of the agreement to recover contractual attorney fees as costs because contractual attorney fees are recoverable as an element of costs under CCP 1033.5. As to prejudgment interest, the motion is DENIED. Plaintiff has alleged that a partner acted inequitably in distributing funds. A prayer for prejudgment interest is proper pursuant to case law cited by Plaintiff. (Luchs v. Ormsby (1959) 171 Cal. App. 2d 377, 388; see also Chesapeake Indus., Inc. v. Togova Enterprises, Inc. (1983) 149 Cal. App. 3d 901, 909.) As to punitive damages, the motion is DENIED. Plaintiff has alleged fraud claims, which constitute tort claims. A prayer for punitive damages is proper under Civil Code 3294(a). = (501/REQ)
